sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.12 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group with trivial soluble radical in which there are Sylow 2-subgroups having non-trivial intersection. Suppose that, for any two Sylow 2-subgroups$P$and$Q$of$G$with$P Q 1$, the index$|P:P Q|$does not exceed$2^n$. Is it then true that$|P| 2^{2n}$? { V.\,V.\,Kabanov} Yes, it is, mod CFSG (V.\,I.\,Zenkov, Algebra and Logic, { 36}, no.\,2 (1997), 93--98).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
